Okay, so Typically step one says frame your point first Okay, and then start your story in other words What happens is that you don't want to just tell a story Without creating a frame to that story. It's almost like the perfect example is like, you know, if you go to a hypnosis show, right? You know, though as you're standing outside They say to you Strange things will happen inside beware Beware of the path they start framing you to feel a certain way Because when you frame something It creates the perception of what someone is about to feel what they should be feeling And it increases likely they will actually buy into the way you want them to feel It's called framing or pre-framing. So when you tell a story typically a story Is meant to especially in the context of business, right? Is you tell a story to illustrate a certain point All right, for example, the the point could be like let me give you a perfect example here would be uh the importance Of stealth of telling stories. Okay, and then I would just say that was my point I could say let me just tell you bottom line is one of the most important things in the world Is to know how to tell a great story because without that it's very hard to capture people's attentions and hold it And you find yourself, you know, really not being a person of power one of charisma, right? Kind of like the way I started giving you a lot of reasons why it's important to know I framed this idea that storytelling is important. In fact, let me tell you a little story about it So you always frame the point first. Okay, you tell someone What's the point you're trying to make before you tell you almost give away the moral of the story Before you tell the story not in some way, but you frame what the morals about you create someone a frame on which to A context for them to understand what you're saying. Okay, that's number one step two Okay, this is a big one don't be afraid to tell a personal story What and again and the reason I say that is because this applies I'm thinking of it in a business setting in other words Obviously when you with friends you wouldn't tell, you know, someone else's you would only tell personal stories That's why you're you're in a business setting But the point is is that I mean it's not friend to friend because you'd only tell personal stories, right? In a business setting you might think that what you know, I just use someone else's story is more powerful Let me tell you what I heard. I heard this great story. I heard this that's not nearly as powerful As looking so let me tell you what happened to me Not someone I heard that happened so it could be one of the clients I've helped or I've served But it can't be oh, let me tell you about another salesman how he helped the one is that's not nearly as powerful The best stories out there are always ones that are told about you and also as you're going to step three now Okay, you always kind of start that story with some sort of pattern interrupt This is something that I do really well, right? I say something's a bit outrageous That would you know, basically what they're like, what the fuck like something that they wouldn't typically hear And that sort of breaks their pattern or kind of grabs their attention like for instance with the I tell a great story about the yacht sinking, right? So you want me to oh my god I only tell you about the story of the yacht sinking right everyone wants to hear that story So I start that story by saying okay, so here's let me tell you the story of yacht sinking The first thing you need to know is that there are actually five phases of a quailude high And it was like what they're like they're not they start to laugh All right, you have the tingle phase and everyone's like they're already like you guys are what they try to connect the dots of what i'm saying To the frame i've created so i frame and i do a pattern interrupt, right? I approach a story from an unusual way And everyone's like what not it interrupts people's patterns and forces them to key in on your words Okay, so when you use a pattern interrupt like your attention grabber It's an unusual thing that makes someone think about way that they actually use that con It's almost like that creating that spinning wheel of death We speak about with a straight line when you apply a tonality in the beginning of a declarative versus, you know phrasing it declarative as a question Okay, is hey john jordan belfort and like who what you create this like stay like what what and so you create Uh, when you do this with a pattern interrupt They're like what like they try to so they start really keen to try to use all their mental energy to key into the story Okay, we'll go on to the next step here step four. Okay is to tell an active story Complete with dialogue What do i mean by that? well the Best story tells in fact the pot test i'm releasing Tomorrow i said i came film last week right the guy the guy patrick you're gonna love this guy right my wife And i was watching it last night. I was watching before i released it right Um, and she's like oh my god. He's a really great storyteller and and i intuitively knew that he was he's a very you know he's a well-known guy and um I was saying watch the way he speaks when he tells a story What he does he always brings you into the moment And he uses active dialogue He actually says you know and she turned to me and she said And he recreates now obviously very often. You don't know you can't remember the exact words from a story from 13 years ago So you're sort of you know using a bit of poetic license here, but you use active dialogue In other words you bring so you know i was and i was sitting down with the i ng the jaren form And i was sitting down in the club and someone said to me Hey buddy you might and you actually Tell the story using the dialogue. It's not just oh, then the guy got mad at me and started yelling about that I should be quiet. No the guy turns around says shut the fuck up You actually use active dialogue We tell a story with dialogue that is absolutely crucial to telling a great story if you just Tell a story passively Where you're approximating what was said builds and he you know he turned around the guys were mad. They were yelling they were Not they were yelling. It's they yelled What they're yelling and they're yelling like now it's happen So you bring someone into the moment of the story with active dialogue And you play the roles of multiple characters within that story And if you with a little bit of trains very easy, especially if you're studying the straight line is to use tonality It's sort of on different you know one character's got one tonality another character's got another right? Let's move on to the step number five Okay, and again this comes into telling your story in the moment so When you tell a story as if it happened in the past It loses its power You want to have the people who are listening? Hanging on your every word by bringing them in the moment You get it. See it's a very big difference between saying yeah three years ago They were doing it for saying you know and i'm walking i'm walking down the road. I look to my left I see in the window this sign the woman runs out and says present dialogue You get it? That's that's probably the biggest Secret to telling a great story. There are others. It's just a sure as soon as you take out that active dog the story loses It's alert loses its power. Okay. We want to step number six And this is my one of my favorites of all right, so This one is the devil Is in the details? What do I mean by that? When you tell a story the natural Impulsion is to leave out A lot of the sort of and the curtains had where you read these really weird red curtains with velvety strips And these monograms sticking out on it and the and I walked there was a disgusting smell It smelled putrid and it's also you could feel dripping off the walls and a guy walks in with oily hair He's wearing this cheap rayon shirt thin tie. Okay. He's got a wart in the end And you're like who would want to hear all that shit? Guess what? That's what makes the story great The natural tendency is to actually tell a long story It's still thirsty apart from the hot yogi I say right Is to tell a longer story, but a shallow story Not put in all the details When what people actually want to hear is they want to hear this, you know much deeper Story, but probably a shorter one So you want to really describe out your locations What did the location? What are the place in which the story Took place? What did it look like? What did that feeling what it smelled all five senses need to be described to a point What were the characters how what were they wearing? How did they look? Were they sweating were they laughing were they crying? The devil is in the details As soon as I say this for other four I walk through four people I'll talk about the 12 stratas. I say the 12 guys these guys like they barely fucking, you know Or just out of adolescence. They could you know, they barely acquainted with a razor blade They had more pimples on there, you know guys look at their expressions on their faces glum say I tell you everything about the people That's what great writers do That's what great movie, you know directors do they show you all these great details. They bring it to life remember Whether you're telling a true story or fiction There's still this this element of suspension of disbelief I mean the person's got to buy into the fact that you know, he's listening to your story It's got like he's experiencing it and believing it for himself in the moment, right? That's suspension of disbelief requires details So don't gloss over the details go heavier on the details next step Okay, is then bring the story back around to the lesson Okay, in other words what you want to do is you open as you are nearing the end of the story You want to kind of close it out with the moral of the story? Okay, and now number eight Okay Is to recreate the tonality of the moment and I mentioned that briefly What I mean by that is that when you tell a story Try your best. I know some of us are more animated than others. I try your best to enter And to approach the story that you know not doing impersonation so to speak But try your best to change your voice for characters and to recreate the emotion Remember tonality creates emotion and the purpose of a story Is your painting this picture to create emotion? That's why painting a picture of someone in the future is the future pacing is all about you do it through stories It's a story of how someone feels in the future tonality is going to be crucial So use the correct tonality. Okay. Now those are your eight steps. I want to add on one Especially important bonus step, right? That is Where do you start your story? It's a fucking disaster, right? So the story begins there and ends there if you tell the story like that No one gives a shit Okay, so where do I tell the story? Where do I begin? And boom just like that, right? It becomes the single most abused drug in the history of drugs, right? I'm fucking loving It's the best thing ever like And I'm all the four drew like the sir. I mean like the cerebral palsy phase basically, right? And I'm lying there on the floor and no one's around because it's winter Right and I'm lying there. So I said fuck I'll just be implicit and it holds like oh shit Danny is in my house I'm like, I gotta get home. He's probably crawling around my wife's gonna kill me, right? I turn myself into a barrel I barrel down the stairs and the beauty of quails by like I'm shining through don't get a fucking scratch I'm gonna scratch because the quails protect me. I get in my car, right? And I get behind the wheel I'm crawling there and I do what probably 10 000 a million of the drunks and druggers have done before I said God Please spare me. Just please get me home one piece. I will never do this again. I crawl out. I crawl up to the front door. My wife grabs just what's wrong with you, Danny He's like, I'm gonna take him to the hospital. He's fucking drooling like a monkey down. What the fuck? But anyway, that's how you tell a awesome story And you apply this technique to anything in life guys.
